title: Adjustable hanger device
abstract: An adjustable hanger device including a base plate mountable to a support surface, and an elongated slide bar attached to the base plate and spaced apart, therefrom, The hanger device includes a flexible strap, a first end of the strap is attached to an end of the slide bar, a length of the strap being disposed at a first angle relative to the length of the slide bar. An adjustable fastener is provided to adjustably secure the strap to the slide bar at a point between the first and second ends of the strap, the strap being twisted axially to form a spiral loop, the loop defining an opening disposed at a second angle relative to the length of the side bar; and wherein an implement can be supported via the hanger device by inserting the handle into the loop and allowing the implement to return to a vertical position.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. An adjustable hanger device comprising: a base plate moimtable to a support surface; an elongated slide bar fixedly attached to the base plate; a flexible strap defining first and second ends, the first end of the strap attached to a first end of the slide bar, a length of the strap being disposed at a first angle relative to the length of the slide bar; an adjustable fastener configured to adjustably secure the strap to the slide bar at a point between the first and second ends of the strap; the strap being configured to twist axialiy to form a spiral loop, the loop defining an opening disposed at a second angle relative to the length of the side bar; and wherein an elongated portion of an implement when tilted at the second angle may be inserted into the loop, the strap being sufficiently flexible to permit the implement to be returned to a vertical position whi le the elongated portion of the implement is retained by tightening of the loop about the elongated portion of the implement. 2. The hanger device according to claim 1 wherein the base plate defines a wall mount surface and a slide bar mount, the slide bar mount being disposed at an acute angle relative to the wall mount surface, the slide bar being attached to the slide bar mount. 3. The hanger device according to claim 2 wherein the base plate defines the wall mount surface and the slide bar mounted formed integrally one with other, at least one bend formed along a length of the base plate separates the wall mount surface from the slide bar mount. 4. The hanger device according to claim 1 wherein ihe slide bar is spaced apart from the base plate along a length of the slide bar. 5. The hanger device according to claim 1, further comprising a fastener for attaching the first end of the strap to the first end of the slide bar. 6. The hanger device according to claim 5, wherein the fastener further comprises a keeper engageable with the first end of the slide bar for fixing the angular position of the strap relative to the slide bar. 7. The banger device according to claim 1 wherein the first end of the slide bar defines an angular surface corresponding to the first angle, the keeper being engageable with the angular surface for fixing the angular position of the strap relative to the slide bar. 8. The hanger device according to claim 7 wherein the keeper is channel shaped. 9. The hanger device according to claim 1 wherein the adjustable fastener further comprises a slide member and a spring clip, the slide member being slideably coupled to the slide bar for movement along a length thereof, the slide member defining an opening for receiving the second end of the strap, the spring clip being removably attachable to the slide member for adjustably securing the strap to the slide bar at a point between the first and second ends of the strap. 10. The hanger device according to claim 9 wherein the slide member is tubular. 11. The hanger device according to claim 9 wherein the spring clip defines first and second legs separated one from the other via a first bend extending through a width of the spring clip. 12. The hanger device according to claim 9 wherein the first leg of the spring clip defines a second bend spaced apart from the first bend, an arched portion extending between the first and second bends. 13. The hanger device according to claim 9 wherein the second leg of the spring clip defines a tab disposed at an angle relative to a length of the spring clip, the tab configured to engage a surface of the flexible strap for securing the flexible strap relative to the slide bar. 14. An adjustable hanger device comprising: a base plate mountable to a support surface; an elongated shde bar fixedly attached to the base plate; a flexible strap defining first and second ends, the first end of the strap attached to a first end of the slide bar, a length of the strap being disposed at a first angle relative to the length of the slide bar; an adjustable fastener configured to adjustably secure the strap to the slide bar at a point between the first and second ends of the strap, the adjustable fastener comprising a slide member and a spring clip, the shde member being slideably coupled to the shde bar for movement along a length thereof, the slide member defining an opening for receiving the second end of the strap, the spring clip being releasabiy attachable to the slide member for securing the strap to the slide bar at a point between the first and second ends of the strap. the strap being twisted axially to form a spiral loop, the loop defining an opening disposed at a second angle relative to the length of the side bar; and wherein an elongated portion of an implement when tilted at the second angle may be inserted into the loop, the strap being sufficiently flexible to permit the implement to be returned to a 

 vertical position while the elongated portion of the implement is retained by tightening of the loop about the elongated portion of the implement. 15. An adjustable strap clip for securing a strap to a slide bar, the strap clip comprising: a slide member defining a tubular shape and having an opening extending through a length thereof, the opening defining a width configured to receive a portion of a strap therethrough; the slide member configured to receive a slide bar through the opening, the slide member being slidable along a length of the slide bar; a strap clip comprising a strap defining a first bend through a width thereof the strap including first and second legs extending outwardly from the first bend in generally the same direction, the strap clip being engageable with the slide member for securing the strap to the slide bar. 16. The adjustable strap clip according to claim 15 wherein the first leg of the strap clip defines an arched portion extending between the first bend and a second bend opposing the first bend. 17. The adjustable strap clip according to claim 15 wherein the second leg of the strap clip defines a tab for engaging the strap within the tube and securing the strap between the tab and the slide bar. 18. The adjustable strap clip according to claim 16 wherein the first leg of the strap defines a third bend extending through the width of the strap near and end of the first leg, the third bend defining a lip, the lip being engageable with the slide member at the opening thereof for securing the strap to the slide bar. 19. The adjustable strap clip according to claim 16 wherein the first leg overlies the second leg forming an interior space between the first arid second legs, the arched portion being generaUy convex in a direction away from the interior space.
